meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Scale Your Freelance Agency Fast
content.seo_description: Stop building from scratch. Learn how to leverage a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to eliminate scaling bottlenecks and ship projects faster.
content.answer_block: To scale your freelance business, abandon manual setup. Utilize a production-ready SaaS starter kit to automate auth, database, and billing. By integrating Antigravity for architectural blueprints and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you reduce development time by 80%, allowing you to focus on high-margin feature delivery rather than repetitive boilerplate.
content.intro:
You’re trapped in the 'custom-build treadmill.' Every new client request feels like a ground-up reconstruction, draining your margins and killing your velocity. You aren't scaling; you're just trading hours for code, hitting a glass ceiling where your output is strictly limited by the clock.

But the industry's top 1% don't write boilerplate—they orchestrate it. By treating your codebase as a modular, repeatable product rather than a one-off project, you transform your workflow from a manual labor trap into a high-leverage machine. It’s time to stop coding like a hobbyist and start building like a factory.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Blueprint
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core constraints of your SaaS starter kit to ensure modularity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular SaaS architecture blueprint focusing on Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include clear separation between core auth logic, database schemas, and API route handlers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --architect 'Define folder structure for a modular SaaS starter kit'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Infrastructure
steps.description: Use your blueprint to generate the base files, ensuring consistent naming conventions and patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold the base project structure based on the architectural blueprint. Implement the auth provider and database connection utility.

steps.title: Automate Billing Integration
steps.description: Standardize your Stripe integration to avoid repetitive configuration for every client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable Stripe checkout service that handles subscription webhooks and user-to-customer mapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'integrate stripe webhooks with existing auth user model'

steps.title: Implement Feature Modules
steps.description: Build plug-and-play features that can be dropped into any client project without refactoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular 'Dashboard' component that accepts dynamic data props for multi-tenant analytics.

steps.title: Refine and Optimize for Production
steps.description: Run a final audit to ensure code quality and performance benchmarks are met before deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a production readiness audit on the current codebase, focusing on security vulnerabilities and bundle size optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --audit 'check for security flaws and performance bottlenecks'

pro_tips: Never hardcode environment variables; use a standardized .env.example file that your starter kit automatically enforces.
Maintain a 'Core' repository that you fork for every new client project to keep updates synced via upstream merges.
Abstract your database queries into a 'Data Access Layer' so you can swap ORMs without rewriting your UI logic.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your starter kit modules; if it doesn't have 80% coverage, it isn't ready for a client.
Implement a 'Feature Flag' system early, allowing you to toggle premium modules on or off depending on the client's budget.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is stabilized, document your workflow in a private Wiki and begin templating your most requested features (like Auth0 integration or advanced role-based access control) to further decrease your time-to-market.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start dominating the market. By leveraging these tools, you reclaim 20+ hours per week, allowing you to scale your agency and take on higher-value clients. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
